decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective and conservative first step is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader material to improve lateral heat dissipation away from the hot spot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity by 20% (within the 2.0x limit) enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local temperature gradient under the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though not constrained here
